qas.a: What career field will plan, direct, and execute full spectrum operations in and through cyberspace in support of assigned missions?
What identifies an Airman's additional qualifications and experience?
What prefix identifies enlisted Airmen who are serving in, qualified to serve in, or receiving training to qualify to serve in formal training instructor positions?

What prefix identifies enlisted Airmen who are serving in, qualified to serve in, or receiving formal training to qualify to serve in positions requiring information operations (IO) expertise and knowledge?
What is career group 1?
What characters in an AFSC identify the career field?
What is a functional grouping of personnel requiring the same knowledge and skills?
What characters in an AFSC identify the career field subdivision?
What is a logical grouping of personnel with related work requirements and qualifications?
What does the 4th character in an AFSC identify?

qas.q: Career Field Education and Training Plan (CFETP)
qas.a: What is a comprehensive education and training document that identifies life cycle education and training requirements, training support resources, and minimum core task requirements for each Air Force specialty?
What training does an individual receive for award of the 3-skill level?

What training identifies the mandatory courses, task qualification requirements, career development course (CDC) completion, and correspondence courses required for award of the 5-, 7-, or 9-skill level?

qas.q: The 1BXXX AFCFM, Secretary of the Air Force, Office of Information Dominance and chief information officer (SAF/CIO A6).
qas.a: Who maintains the 1B CFETP?
What skill level is awarded upon retraining acceptance and approval?
What skill level is awarded upon completion of the 1B4 Initial Skills Training?
What skill level is awarded after completing the 1B451 CDC, after the required core Specialty Training Standard (STS) tasks are signed off?
What skill level requires a minimum rank of SSgt?
What skill level requires a minimum rank of SMSgt?
What is the title of the 1B000 code?

What are used in conjunction with AFSC prefixes to match qualified individuals to jobs with special requirements and identify special experience and training not otherwise identified within the personnel data system (PDS)?

Which AFSC deploys, sustains, and manages data, information, and knowledge-sharing services in a fixed and expeditionary environment, to include planning, coordinating, sharing, and controlling an organization's data and information assets?
Which AFSC's core competencies include server operating systems, database administration, network management, configuration management, and web technologies?
Which AFSC administers and manages the overall Information Assurance (IA) program to include Communications Security (COMSEC), Emissions Security (EMSEC) and Computer Security (COMPUSEC) programs?

Which AFSC supervises and performs as computer analyst, coder, tester and manager in the design, development, maintenance, testing, configuration management, and documentation of application software systems?
Which AFSC deploys, sustains, troubleshoots, and repairs standard voice, data, video network, and cryptographic client devices in fixed and deployed environments?
Which AFSC deploys, sustains, troubleshoots, and repairs standard voice, data, and video network infrastructure systems, internet protocol (IP) detection systems and cryptographic equipment?

Which AFSC deploys, sustains, troubleshoots, and repairs standard radio frequency wireless, line-of-sight, beyond line-of-sight, wideband, ground-based satellite, and encryption transmission devices and intrusion detection systems in a fixed and deployed environment?

Which AFSC analyzes requirements and requests frequencies to support terrestrial, aircraft and space systems and coordinate radio, radar, land, and other electromagnetic radiating or receiving requirements?

Which AFSC provides C2 capabilities through installation, maintenance, fault isolation, and reconstitution of fixed cable and wireless distribution systems, local area networks (LANs), and wide area networks (WANs) in support of tactical and strategic operations?

Which AFSC is "all-source," relying on information from all disciplines to develop, evaluate, and provide intelligence information to support training, exercise, and contingency operations at all levels?
Which AFSC is involved in acquiring, identifying, processing, analyzing, and reporting electromagnetic emissions?
Which AFSC analyzes, disseminates, and exploits intelligence information to develop global communications structures for targeting, exploitation, and situational awareness?
Which AFSC detects, and maintains surveillance on low orbiting and deep space satellite vehicles using active and passive space surveillance systems?
Which Operations Officer Plans, organizes and performs Net Ops to include establishment, operations, information assurance and defense in support of joint, national and AF objectives?
Which Operations Officer is equivalent to 1B4X1, and plans, organizes and performs network defense, exploitation and attack in support of joint, national and AF objectives?
Which officer career field provides the necessary threat analysis of potential adversaries and targets, as well as threat analysis to friendly assets (networks, satellites, etc.)?
Which officer career field operates and manages surveillance, intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) launch and space lift, ballistic space warning, and satellite C2?

qas.q: flag and general officers in the U.S. Armed Forces
qas.a: Within the DoD, SES personnel are considered analogous to what?
Who provide a flexible workforce dedicated to supplementing the civilian and military workforce?

Who are responsible for ensuring all aspects of the legal contract are fulfilled, as well as working with the contractor to guarantee that their personnel are qualified, pass background checks, and have access to the areas required to perform their duties to fulfill the contract?
